meta.title: Component showcase
meta.subtitle: Browse and try every UI primitive, interaction pattern, and performance demo that ships with the template.
meta.devOnlyNotice: Preview is available in development only — these routes are excluded from production builds.

button.title: Button
button.description: Variant + size matrix with the motion/react micro-interactions baked into the primitive.
button.asChildHint: Render as an anchor while keeping button styles

card.title: Card
card.description: Plain, hoverable and structured (header + content + footer) variants. The hoverable variant lifts subtly on mouse enter when prefers-reduced-motion is off.
card.plainBody: Borders, padding and tokenised surfaces with zero extra config.
card.hoverableBody: Hover me — the whole card lifts a few pixels.
card.structuredBody: Compose CardHeader + CardContent + CardFooter to keep typography consistent.

tabs.title: Tabs
tabs.description: Radix Tabs with the active trigger styled via data-[state=active]. Three panels share the same trigger row.
tabs.accountBody: Profile, billing, and connected services live here.
tabs.passwordBody: Rotate credentials and review active sessions.

theme.title: Theme tokens
theme.description: Every primitive paints with CSS custom properties exposed under var(--color-*). Switch the theme from the header to see all tokens flip in lock-step.

i18n.title: i18n
i18n.description: Feature copy lives under src/features/<name>/i18n/<lng>.json. Each namespace is its own lazy chunk. Switch the language to see the whole tree re-translate.
i18n.tryItHint: Hint: open the language switcher in the header.
i18n.sampleBody: This block reads from the examples namespace and updates instantly when you switch languages.

animation.title: Animation primitives
animation.description: Token-controlled timing for spinners, pulses and entrance animations. All respect prefers-reduced-motion.
animation.hoverHint: Hover the box to trigger the spring lift.

toaster.title: Toast
toaster.description: Design-driven Sonner toaster with configurable position. The toast styling follows the active design preset automatically; switch positions to preview placement.

dialog.title: Dialog
dialog.description: Radix Dialog with motion/react enter/exit transitions, conditionally portalled so the subtree fully unmounts on close. Four flavours below cover every common Modal shape.

basics.title: Basic flavours
basics.description: Informational, form-style and destructive-confirmation Modals — the three shapes you reach for 80% of the time.
basics.destructiveDetail: Make sure you have a backup before you proceed.
basics.formDescription: Notes are stored locally and never sent anywhere.

sizes.title: Width matrix
sizes.description: Override DialogContent's default max-width with a single Tailwind class. Use sm for confirmation prompts, md/lg for forms, xl for content surfaces.

nested.title: Nested dialogs
nested.description: A confirmation Modal opened from inside another Modal. Radix's portal stack handles z-index; AnimatePresence handles independent exit animations.
nested.parentBody: Choose a workspace action below — destructive actions open a confirmation Modal on top of this one.
nested.confirmBody: This permanently removes the workspace and every artefact inside it. There is no undo.

fullscreen.title: Fullscreen flavour
fullscreen.description: Viewport-filling Modal for mobile shells where the modal IS the page. Drops the centred-card chrome and runs edge-to-edge.
fullscreen.body: The Modal owns the whole viewport.
fullscreen.bodyDetail: Use this shape for image cropping, multi-step wizards, or any flow where chrome would steal attention. The header / footer split keeps the action bar reachable on tall mobile screens.

sheet.title: Sheet
sheet.description: Side-anchored drawers built on the same Radix primitives as Dialog but with edge-locked motion. Use Sheet when the surface should slide in from a screen edge — Dialog when it should centre over the page.

directions.title: Four edges
directions.description: The same Sheet primitive accepts any side via the `side` prop. Mobile drawers usually anchor left or right; quick filters / pickers anchor bottom; status panels anchor top.

scroll.title: Scrollable body
scroll.description: When the Sheet contents overflow the viewport, the SheetBody scrolls independently — the header / footer stay pinned so primary actions never sit below the fold.

command.title: Command palette
command.description: Searchable, keyboard-first menu built on cmdk. Wrap it in our animated Dialog (CommandDialog) for the classic ⌘K experience, or render Command inline for static menus.
command.shortcutHint: Press ⌘K (or Ctrl+K) anywhere on this page to open the palette.

signInModal.title: Sign-in modal
signInModal.description: The auth feature's modal is mounted once at the provider level (SignInModalMount in app/providers.tsx) and openable from any route via useOpenSignInModal(). Trigger it below to preview both modes.
signInModal.mountedNoteBody: The Modal is already live in this app — the buttons below just call openModal() on the auth store. Look for the topbar Sign in button on the right to see the same trigger inline.
signInModal.modeTabHint: Use the tabs inside the modal to switch between sign in and sign up; OAuth providers are wired to the Mock service in this preview.

motion.title: Motion
motion.description: motion/react primitives wired to common UI moments — entrance, hover, tap, layout. Toggle prefers-reduced-motion in your OS to see the static fallbacks.

performance.title: Performance
performance.description: Three runtime diagnostics demos: Core Web Vitals, virtualised lists, and lazy-loaded chunks. Numbers refresh every two seconds.

webVitals.title: Core Web Vitals
webVitals.description: Live readings from the web-vitals library. Numbers may be — until the underlying observer fires (LCP needs a layout, INP needs an interaction).
webVitals.interactHint: Click anywhere on the page to register an INP sample.

virtualList.title: Virtual list
virtualList.description: 10,000 rows rendered by @tanstack/react-virtual. Only the rows in the viewport are mounted, so scrolling stays smooth regardless of dataset size.
virtualList.scrollHint: Scroll the list — only visible rows are mounted.

lazyLoad.title: Lazy load
lazyLoad.description: Trigger a dynamic import and watch the Suspense fallback while the chunk is fetched. Slow Network tab in DevTools to extend the visible window.
lazyLoad.loadedBody: This component lives in its own JS chunk; it only downloaded once you asked for it.
